item.title: Phones in Hand, India’s Young Protesters Blast Through Modi’s Media Control
item.author: Mujib Mashal
item.content:encoded: Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s tightly constructed image is being punctured as a Gen Z-led protest movement uses videos and memes to seize the digital narrative.

item.title: The Cost of Trump’s New Tariffs, and the $100 Million Popcorn Bucket Market
item.author: Tracy Mumford, Will Jarvis, Margaret Kadifa, Jake Lucas, Ian Stewart, Alex Travelli and Anupreeta Das
item.content:encoded: Plus, the Friday news quiz.

item.title: Brent Crude Oil Prices Top $100 as Conflict With Iran Drags On
item.author: Laura Stevens
item.content:encoded: Stocks in Asia were down following a sell-off in the U.S. markets over soaring oil prices, rising borrowing costs and worries about the technology industry.

item.title: Iran War and Trump’s Tariffs Threaten a Resilient U.S. Economy
item.author: Tony Romm
item.content:encoded: As renewed fighting with Iran sends oil and gas prices soaring, President Trump has imposed new global tariffs that could push up prices further.

item.title: How a Grieving Trump Decided to Back Lindsey Graham’s Sister for Senate
item.author: Emily Davies
item.content:encoded: The president initially backed a little-known congressman. Then he talked to Gov. Henry McMaster and Ms. Graham herself.

item.title: Democrats Prepare for Possible Election Clashes With Federal Government
item.author: Nick Corasaniti
item.content:encoded: Top officials overseeing state elections and law enforcement are stepping up plans to prepare for what they worry could be interference in the midterms.

item.title: Is the Amaury Family, Owners of the Tour de France, Hurting Cycling?
item.author: David Segal
item.content:encoded: The Amaurys of Paris own the race, but won’t share any of the $170 million in media revenue. Sponsors and riders say their resistance is dooming the sport.

item.title: ‘We’re Trapped in Hell’: Iranians Say Life Is Put On Hold by Months of War
item.author: Leily Nikounazar and Erika Solomon
item.content:encoded: Devastated by cycles of violence and economic crisis, many are giving up on work and their futures as the United States batters Iran again.

item.title: Platner’s Top Campaign Strategist on What Went Wrong
item.author: Ezra Klein, Rollin Hu, Annie Galvin and Marie Cascione
item.content:encoded: Morris Katz helped Zohran Mamdani win, and then tried to do the same for Graham Platner. What has he learned?

item.title: Venezuela’s Earthquakes Show Wealth Divide, Even in Death
item.author: Emma Bubola and Adriana Loureiro Fernandez
item.content:encoded: Latin America’s deep-seated inequalities prevailed over the grimly equalizing force of the Venezuela earthquakes, and set even the dead apart.

item.title: In ‘The Odyssey’ and ‘Oppenheimer,’ Nolan Knows Civilization Is at Stake
item.author: Alissa Wilkinson
item.content:encoded: In very different films, the director’s penchant for playing with time is in service to a larger concern about human bonds across eras.

item.title: Voters Think All Politicians Are a Little Corrupt, but Trump Might Be Pushing It
item.author: Charles Homans
item.content:encoded: There’s a difference between ordinary influence peddling and the first family’s outright self-enrichment.

item.title: Can This New Enzyme Turn Back the Clock in Bodily Tissues?
item.author: K. R. Callaway
item.content:encoded: In a recent study, scientists devised a way of reversing the buildup of compounds that lead to some age-related diseases.

item.title: Our Vast Collective Jeffrey Epstein Failure
item.author: Rachel Louise Snyder
item.content:encoded: We need to hold accountable the web of accomplices who enabled his crimes.

item.title: In Kyrgyzstan, Girls Compete at Kok-Boru, a Game Like Polo
item.author: Alexander M. Thompson and Sevindj Nurkiyazova
item.content:encoded: Here’s what happened when some teenage girls dared to compete in the dangerous sport of “kok-boru.”

item.title: Faced With Heat and Wildfires, France Turns to Spain to Learn How to Cope
item.author: Mark Landler
item.content:encoded: A French government delegation will travel to its southern neighbor to learn how it copes with long spells of hot weather.

item.title: Jimmy Fallon Pokes Fun at Trump’s ‘Pinky Herman’
item.author: Trish Bendix
item.content:encoded: “You got to give Trump credit: He misspeaks in ways nobody ever thought possible,” Fallon said of the president’s calling the famous man-child character “Pinky,” instead of “Pee-wee,” Herman.

item.title: Trump Administration Imposed New Tariffs of Around 10% on Over 80 Nations
item.author: Ana Swanson
item.content:encoded: The administration launched new duties tied to countries’ imports of goods made with forced labor, replacing a 10 percent tariff immediately after it expired.

item.title: Trump’s Global Tariffs: What’s the Latest on the Trade War
item.author: Tony Romm, Edison Wu and Lazaro Gamio
item.content:encoded: The president has unveiled a complicated system to forge ahead with his trade war, after the Supreme Court stuck down his original levies.

item.title: Number of U.S. Troop Deaths in Iran War Appears Lower on Pentagon Website
item.author: Helene Cooper, Lara Jakes and John Ismay
item.content:encoded: Three military officials and a Pentagon spokesman gave conflicting explanations for why the number of Americans killed in the Iran war had changed.

item.title: A Fallen Soldier’s Angst, Love and Longing to Return Home From the Iran War
item.author: Jacey Fortin
item.content:encoded: Sgt. Michael Emmanuel Swinton was killed in recent days, one of 18 U.S. soldiers who have lost their lives since the beginning of the war in Iran.

item.title: Prominent Indian Activist Breaks Hunger Strike After 26 Days
item.author: Pranav Baskar
item.content:encoded: The activist, Sonam Wangchuk, said that he had broken his fast “after long negotiations” with officials and “in view of possible violence in the country.”

item.title: U.S. Withdraws Subpoenas Issued to New York Times Journalists
item.author: Michael M. Grynbaum and Jonah E. Bromwich
item.content:encoded: A federal judge raised questions about the government’s handling of the subpoenas, which The Times called an attempt to intimidate the free press.

item.title: Lebanon has a chance to break free of Hizbullah
item.content:encoded: With better government, it could become a business hub once again

item.title: Politics
Business
Cartoon: America’s trouble getting out of the Iran conflict
item.content:encoded: A lighter take on the news

item.title: Should you be afraid of Elon Musk?
item.content:encoded: Artificial intelligence is charging ahead. Not even its creators know how to keep up

item.title: Prediction markets need better rules
item.content:encoded: Their capricious settlement mechanisms are putting off serious investors

item.title: Donald Trump’s Saudi deal risks nuclear proliferation
item.content:encoded: It could have consequences far beyond the Middle East

item.title: Britain’s troublingly nostalgic new prime minister
item.content:encoded: In an era of change, Andy Burnham promises to rewind the clock

item.title: When a president stops pretending that voters count, disaster beckons
item.content:encoded: Tiny Nicaragua offers a cautionary tale for democrats everywhere

item.title: Cover Story newsletter: It’s too darn hot
item.content:encoded: An exclusive look at how we designed our cover

item.title: Cartoon: Continuing uncertainty over the Strait of Hormuz
item.content:encoded: A lighter take on the news

item.title: China’s rulers have a woman problem
item.content:encoded: Antagonism between the sexes does nothing to address China’s demographic problems

item.title: America should stop making it so hard to have fun
item.content:encoded: Bureaucracy is nightlife’s toughest bouncer

item.title: Politics
Business
Donald Trump’s gutting of the Department of Justice
item.content:encoded: What the degraded institution means for America

item.title: It’s too darn hot. Blame global dimming
item.content:encoded: Earth is absorbing a lot more sunshine

item.title: Donald Trump’s blind alley
item.content:encoded: America’s president looks bereft of good options for solving the stand-off in the Gulf

item.title: How to make AI safe—and lessen dependence on America and China
item.content:encoded: State-backed efforts to catch up with frontier models are doomed

item.title: Cover Story newsletter: The man who would change Russia
item.content:encoded: An exclusive look at how we designed our cover

item.title: Two cheers for Trump Accounts
item.content:encoded: The grubby scheme contains the seeds of a good idea

item.title: England needs fewer council homes, not more
item.content:encoded: Andy Burnham’s plan is no way to ease the housing crisis

item.title: A no-brainer for protecting your brain
item.content:encoded: One simple vaccination may dramatically reduce the risk of dementia

item.title: Who is capable of evil?
item.content:encoded: Stop lowering the age of criminal responsibility

item.title: Politics
Business
Cartoon: The real threat to global growth
item.content:encoded: A lighter take on the news
